req.title: Run payroll — variant 2
req.statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path payroll run producing correct pay-stub records and audit entry [EP], (2) conflict guard when a run is already in progress [NEG], (3) atomic rollback on internal failure [DT], (4) idempotency under retry — submitting the same run-id twice must not double-commit [BVA boundary at exactly 2 identical submissions], and (5) state-transition from 'idle' -> 'in_progress' -> 'completed' [STC]. Five tests are provided to satisfy the minimum-three requirement while achieving meaningful coverage across all five heuristics.

tests.name: test_payroll_run_happy_path_creates_stubs_and_audit
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as hr-admin (token fixture)
At least one active employee exists in the system
No payroll run is currently in progress for the target period
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with valid period payload
Poll or assert synchronous 201 response
GET /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} to confirm state=completed
GET /api/pay-stubs?run_id={run_id} to verify records
GET /api/audit-log?entity=payroll-run&entity_id={run_id} to verify audit entry
expected: Pay-stub count equals active employee count
Each stub has gross_pay > 0, deductions >= 0, net_pay = gross_pay - deductions
Audit log contains one entry for this run_id
trace: R-WEB-073: payroll computes gross, deductions, net for each active employee
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_payroll_run_happy_path_creates_stubs_and_audit(
    hr_admin_client,      # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    active_employee_ids,  # <fixture> list of active employee IDs
    payroll_period,       # <fixture> returns {"period": "YYYY-MM"}
    api_base,             # <fixture> base URL string
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs", json=payroll_period)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, resp.text
    run_id = resp.json()["run_id"]

    run = hr_admin_client.get(f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}").json()
    assert run["state"] == "completed"

    stubs = hr_admin_client.get(
        f"{api_base}/api/pay-stubs", params={"run_id": run_id}
    ).json()["items"]
    assert len(stubs) == len(active_employee_ids)
    for stub in stubs:
        assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
        assert stub["deductions"] >= 0
        assert stub["net_pay"] == pytest.approx(
            stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"]
        )

    audit = hr_admin_client.get(
        f"{api_base}/api/audit-log",
        params={"entity": "payroll-run", "entity_id": run_id},
    ).json()["items"]
    assert len(audit) >= 1

tests.name: test_payroll_run_conflict_returns_409_when_run_in_progress
preconditions: A payroll run for the target period is currently in state=in_progress (seeded via fixture)
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with the same period while one is in_progress
Assert response status and error body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating run already in progress
trace: R-WEB-073: 409 if another run is in progress
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_conflict_returns_409_when_run_in_progress(
    hr_admin_client,
    payroll_period,
    in_progress_run,  # <fixture> seeds a run in state=in_progress and yields run_id
    api_base,
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs", json=payroll_period
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body
    conflict_indicator = body.get("error", body.get("code", ""))
    assert "in_progress" in conflict_indicator.lower() or "conflict" in conflict_indicator.lower()

tests.name: test_payroll_run_atomic_rollback_on_internal_failure
preconditions: Fault-injection header or environment flag enabled to simulate compute failure mid-run
No prior payroll run exists for the target period
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with fault-injection header
Assert HTTP 500
GET /api/pay-stubs?run_id={run_id} to verify no partial stubs committed
GET /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} to verify no run record persisted or state=rolled_back
expected: Zero pay-stub records exist for the attempted run_id
Payroll run record is absent or in state=rolled_back (not partially completed)
trace: R-WEB-073: 500 rolled back atomically on failure
R-WEB-073: a failed run shall not partially commit

tests.name: test_payroll_run_idempotent_on_duplicate_submission
preconditions: No payroll run exists for the target period
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with Idempotency-Key header — first request
Record run_id from first response
POST /api/payroll-runs with identical Idempotency-Key header — second request (boundary: exactly 2 identical submissions)
Compare run_ids and pay-stub counts
expected: Both requests return 201 or second returns 200/201 with identical run_id
Pay-stub count after second call equals count after first call (no duplication)
Exactly one audit entry exists for the run_id
trace: R-WEB-073: action must be idempotent under retry
BVA: boundary at N=2 identical submissions — the minimum count that could expose double-commit
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_idempotent_on_duplicate_submission(
    hr_admin_client,
    payroll_period,
    idempotency_key,  # <fixture> returns a stable UUID string per test
    api_base,
):
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    r1 = hr_admin_client.post(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs", json=payroll_period, headers=headers
    )
    assert r1.status_code in (200, 201), r1.text
    run_id_1 = r1.json()["run_id"]

    r2 = hr_admin_client.post(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs", json=payroll_period, headers=headers
    )
    assert r2.status_code in (200, 201), r2.text
    run_id_2 = r2.json()["run_id"]

    assert run_id_1 == run_id_2, "Idempotency violated: different run_ids returned"

    stubs = hr_admin_client.get(
        f"{api_base}/api/pay-stubs", params={"run_id": run_id_1}
    ).json()["items"]
    stub_ids = [s["id"] for s in stubs]
    assert len(stub_ids) == len(set(stub_ids)), "Duplicate stubs detected"

    audit = hr_admin_client.get(
        f"{api_base}/api/audit-log",
        params={"entity": "payroll-run", "entity_id": run_id_1},
    ).json()["items"]
    assert len(audit) == 1, f"Expected 1 audit entry, found {len(audit)}"

tests.name: test_payroll_run_state_transitions_idle_to_completed
preconditions: No payroll run exists for the target period (state: idle / absent)
actions: Confirm no run record exists for the period (state=idle/absent)
POST /api/payroll-runs to trigger run (state should transition to in_progress then completed)
GET /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} immediately after POST
Poll until state=completed or assert synchronous completion
Verify terminal state is completed and not in_progress
expected: Before POST: run record absent or state=idle
After POST: state eventually equals completed
State never regresses from completed back to in_progress
trace: STC: covers idle -> in_progress -> completed transition arc
